message:
test(frankenpandas): add Window Operations integration test (br-4ak09 / fd90.165)

fd90.165: the README's 'Window Operations' section (lines 474-487)
showcases core rolling/expanding/ewm chains but no integration test
exercised them via prelude::*. Locking in coverage now.

Fix: add 'readme_window_operations_compiles_and_runs' which mirrors
the example. Builds a 100-element numeric Series and verifies:
  - series.rolling(30, None).mean()? returns 100 values
  - series.rolling(20, Some(5)).std()? — Some(min_periods) Option arg
  - series.expanding(None).max()? — None min_periods Option arg
  - series.ewm(Some(20.0), None).mean()? — span Option arg

Resample is intentionally skipped here because it requires a
datetime-like index; the rest of the chain runs cleanly against the
numeric default-Int64-indexed Series.

Verified:
- cargo test -p frankenpandas — 7 lib + 11 integration + 2 doctests
  all pass

Pure additive test. No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-44vd","title":"[HIGH] fp-conformance 8 cascading aggregate tests fail on opaque assert_is_green; need fixture-level surface","description":"Workspace test (rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets) shows 351 passed / 8 failed in fp-conformance. The 8 failures all panic with terse messages like 'assertion failed: report.is_green()' or 'CI PIPELINE: FAILED (0/1 gates passed)' — the underlying fixture-level mismatches (37 cases per packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases) are buried inside the report payload only when {report:?} is included.

Affected tests (all in crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs):
- ci_gate_g6_conformance_evaluates (line 29042)
- ci_pipeline_conformance_only_runs_and_reports (line 29063)
- differential_suite_produces_structured_drift (line 27256)
- failure_forensics_report_clean_when_all_pass (line 28519)
- e2e_suite_runs_full_pipeline (line 28054)
- grouped_reports_are_partitioned_per_packet (line 23210)
- packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases (line 20547)
- differential_all_packets_green (line 27457)

Fix: replace the bare assert!(report.is_green()) at each site with a structured failure dump that emits per-case (packet_id, case_id, mismatch_class, mismatch) when report.failed > 0. Pattern already used at 20943 in packet_filter_runs_groupby_aggregate_matrix_packet ('expected report green: {report:?}'). Apply the same pattern to all 8 sites. Bonus: write the dump to a JSONL artifact under artifacts/ so the reaper can ingest it.

Impact: turns 8 'opaque cascade' failures into actionable per-case records that downstream agents can pick off without re-running each top-level test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:04:35.846664619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:35:45.419463531Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T08:35:45.417359367Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

  series.rolling(30, None).mean()?
  series.rolling(252, Some(20)).std()?
  series.expanding(None).max()?
  series.ewm(Some(20.0), None).mean()?
  series.resample('M').sum()?

These are core time-series / windowing primitives but no integration test exercises them via prelude::*. The Rolling / Expanding / Ewm / Resample types aren't even re-exported from frankenpandas (return types only), so users rely entirely on method chaining.

Fix: add 'readme_window_operations_compiles_and_runs' which mirrors the example. Build a 100-element numeric Series and verify each window chain produces the expected length output.  {"id":"frankenpandas-4clx","title":"[HIGH] release: 12 crates at 0.1.0 with no publish workflow or semver policy — library is structurally un-releasable","description":"Release / publish workflow + versioning audit.

**Observation.**
- All 12 crates sit at version = "0.1.0" (verified via grep). No crate has been bumped since the workspace was initialized.
- No `publish = false` set anywhere. Cargo's default is `publish = true`, which means any developer running `cargo publish -p fp-types` today would push 0.1.0 to crates.io. A moment later, `cargo publish -p fp-columnar` fails because fp-columnar has `fp-types = { path = "../fp-types" }` and path-dependencies can't be published. The publish ordering is undocumented.
- .github/workflows/ has exactly ONE file: ci.yml. No release.yml, no publish.yml, no tag-driven workflow.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` section per crate — there's a workspace-root CHANGELOG but no per-crate versioning story.
- No semver policy documented. The workspace edition is "2024" (Rust 2024 edition, nightly-only), so MSRV is unpinned — but semver needs a user-facing stability story.

**Consequence.**
- First release is impossible today without manual ceremony: 12 Cargo.toml bumps, dependency-path rewrite to `version = "..."` pinned cross-crate refs, cargo publish each in topological order, tag the commit, update Cargo.lock, repeat across all downstream crates. Any misstep breaks the publish.
- frankenpandas-diic bead (PyO3 bindings) depends on the core crates being publishable — the Python wheel pipeline needs `frankenpandas = "X.Y.Z"` from crates.io.
- Multi-agent swarm adds commits faster than any manual release process can keep up. If release is manual, releases never happen; users stay on git dependencies.
- Security fixes (future CVE patches) can't ship quickly because the pipeline doesn't exist.

**Severity.** HIGH — the product is structurally un-releasable today despite being 3171-tests-green. README markets "Add to your Cargo.toml: frankenpandas = { path = 'crates/frankenpandas' }" which is a path dep, not a released version. Users can't consume the library the way the README implies.

**Fix shape:**

1. Workspace-level versioning:
   - Every crate's Cargo.toml [package] version inherits from workspace via `version.workspace = true`.
   - Single source of truth in root Cargo.toml [workspace.package] version = "0.1.0" → bumped once per release.
2. Semver policy in CONTRIBUTING.md (sibling bead): pre-1.0 = minor bumps may break, patch bumps won't; post-1.0 = strict semver.
3. Cross-crate dep version pinning: [workspace.dependencies] gets one fp-types = { version = "0.1.0", path = "crates/fp-types" } entry each, so published crates carry a versioned dep and path-lookup happens only during local dev.
4. Release workflow `.github/workflows/release.yml`:
   - Trigger: git tag matching `v*`.
   - Steps: validate semver bump matches commit messages, run full CI suite, `cargo publish -p <crate>` in topological order (fp-types → fp-columnar → fp-index → fp-runtime → fp-types → fp-expr → fp-groupby → fp-join → fp-io → fp-conformance → frankenpandas), attach CHANGELOG.md diff to GitHub Release.
5. `cargo-release` (external tool) or `release-plz` (GitHub Action) automates the topological publish. Install in CI; configure via release-plz.toml.
6. Per-crate CHANGELOG files auto-generated by release-plz from conventional-commit prefixes (feat/fix/chore/etc — the prefixes are already in use based on recent git log).

Blast radius:
- 12 Cargo.toml edits (version.workspace = true flip, +1 line each).
- workspace Cargo.toml: add [workspace.package] version, add [workspace.dependencies] entries for each fp-* crate.
- 1 new .github/workflows/release.yml (~100 lines).
- 1 new release-plz.toml (~30 lines).
- 1 new section in CONTRIBUTING.md (covered by sibling CODEOWNERS bead).

Prerequisites:
- br-frankenpandas-36qc (cargo-audit) + br-frankenpandas-0l5r (cross-platform CI) ideally green before 0.1.0 ships — you don't want to publish a 0.1.0 that's untested on Windows.
- br-frankenpandas-1d9y (nightly pin) — MSRV claim can't happen without a pinned toolchain.

Once all prerequisites + this bead land, first release becomes `git tag v0.1.0 && git push --tags`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:30:47.204682706Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:07:02.551624939Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:07:02.549092304Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["publish","release","review","semver"]}
